notes: Spec asserts: session stays alive past readyTTL, state remains ready during idle, next user prompt resumes ready→working. Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 15s duration_at_least_ms below cleanly exceeds the TTL. If the daemon's PID-liveness check regresses, the idle_window invariants fire (no transcript_removed, no flicker to working). Observations omit model (no prior gemini recording to fix the exact normalized string); cost+tokens nonzero still run.

text: Session appears in ready (pre-session or session id, whichever arrives first)
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL; state remains ready during idle
Next user prompt: ready → working resumes normally
Second turn completes back to ready
